params.text:
Why slips, trips and falls matter

Slips and trips are the single most common cause of injury in UK workplaces, and the Health and Safety Executive (HSE) records them as a leading source of major injuries every year. In a care setting the risk touches two very different groups: the staff who move around the home or a person's own house, and the people receiving care, who may be older or frail and much more likely to be seriously hurt by a fall.

The good news is that almost all of these incidents are preventable. This course looks at both sides of the problem so you can keep yourself safe and help protect the people you support.

  • How to spot and control slip and trip hazards at work
  • Why older and frail people fall, and how to reduce the risk
  • What to do calmly and correctly if someone falls
  • How to record and report falls so lessons are learned

The law behind it

The Health and Safety at Work etc. Act 1974 places a duty on employers to protect the health and safety of employees and of anyone else affected by their work – including the people you care for and their visitors. As an employee you also have a legal duty to take reasonable care of yourself and others, and to co-operate with your employer's safety arrangements.

The Care Quality Commission (CQC) expects providers to keep people safe from avoidable harm, and falls prevention is a key part of delivering safe, good-quality care.

params.question: Slips and trips are one of the most common causes of workplace injury in the UK.

l10n.wrongAnswerMessage: Actually this is true. The HSE reports slips and trips as the most common cause of workplace injury in the UK.
l10n.correctAnswerMessage: Correct. The HSE consistently reports slips and trips as the most common cause of injury in UK workplaces.
